creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Stefan B. from comment #14)

Sorry to hear about your frustrations.  FreeBSD certainly has some limitations, like all OSs, but FWIW, I actually switched from Linux to FreeBSD for reliability reasons.  I spent 20 years professionally managing a variety of Linux distros on a variety of workstation hardware and HPC clusters, and always had problems with systems freezing solid (about once a month on typical hardware).  Running FreeBSD on the same hardware resulted in almost no crashes, and of the few that did happen, most were traced to hardware problems.  This was important to us, since we had users running scientific analyses that could take weeks to complete and could not be easily resumed after a crash.

In my experience, the limitations of FreeBSD tend to be more on the cosmetic side, rather than systemic, and I've always been able to work around them.  That doesn't mean they aren't serious problems for other use cases, so switching to Linux may have been the right move for you.

In any case, sorry to see you go, and thanks for your efforts to improve FreeBSD.  You've undoubtedly left a bit less work for the the rest of us to deal with.
